question.text: For gifting shares to a person resident outside India, prior RBI approval is needed, and the gift shall not exceed what percentage of the paid-up capital of the Indian company?

answer.text: 5 per cent of the paid-up capital
2 per cent of the paid-up capital
10 per cent of the paid-up capital
15 per cent of the paid-up capital

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A person resident in India who intends to transfer any security by way of gift to a person resident outside India requires prior RBI approval, and the gift does not exceed 5 per cent of the paid-up capital of the Indian company / each series of debentures / each mutual fund scheme. **The Trap:** This 5% limit applies to gifts, not sales. Additionally, the transferor and transferee must be close relatives as defined in Section 6 of the Companies Act, 2013, and the sectoral cap should not be breached. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Three conditions for gift transfer: (a) not exceeding 5% of paid-up capital, (b) sectoral cap not breached, (c) donor and donee must be close relatives under Companies Act.
